data.executiveSummary: The incident was caused by Redis connection timeouts leading to high error rates on the qa-login service. Despite moderate CPU usage and acceptable memory, the session store retrieval failure in Redis led to widespread login issues.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The qa-login service encountered multiple 'LoginException' errors due to session store retrieval failures because of Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.

evidenceChain: Detected multiple 'LoginException' entries in Loki logs for qa-login service.
HTTP request duration metrics indicate elevated request durations and errors.
The historical incident KAN-6 involved similar Redis connection timeouts.

timeline.event: LoginException logged in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.
LoginException logged in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.

data.impact: Users faced login failures due to inability to successfully retrieve sessions, leading to a high error rate of 31.85%.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the Redis instance similar to the resolution in incident KAN-6 to resolve orphaned connections.
Implement monitoring and alerts for Redis connection latency to preemptively identify future issues.
Conduct a detailed review of Redis configurations to ensure optimal connection handling and reduce timeout occurrences.
